358.reason: [This line tries to look up "&&" or "||" in doubleCharOperators using a string built from two identical chars, but then uses it to fetch a LexType; however, the token value being created is only the single char (e.g., "&"), but the type is incorrectly taken as if it were the double-char operator. Moreover, doubleCharOperators.get(right) returns a valid LexType (e.g., AND for "&&"), but the actual token value is just "&", which mismatches the expected representation. More critically, this code path still returns a token instead of skipping or handling the error properly—violating the requirement that erroneous inputs should only produce error output and no tokens.]
